body:
Implementation plan complete for BIN-121 (Chapter Pacing Board). Plan added to issue description.

**Summary of approach:**
- New `pacing` ViewMode added to ViewModeSwitch
- 6 new components: PacingBoard shell, PacingChart (horizontal bar chart), TensionTagRow (per-scene tag pills), AlertPanel (3 deterministic alert rules), SnapshotPanel (before/after comparison)
- New types in `src/types/pacing.ts` and lib in `src/lib/pacing.ts`
- Tension tags stored in localStorage `story-{storyId}-tension-tags`; dismissed alerts in `story-{storyId}-dismissed-alerts`; snapshot in sessionStorage
- 3 chart band colors (green/yellow/red) defined as local CSS vars in PacingChart.module.css only — only non-grayscale exception in the app
- Jump-to-scene switches viewMode to corkboard with focusedSceneId set

**Files touched:** 12 new files, 3 modified

Assign to: Code Monkey

Design spec complete. Pacing Board spec covers: chapter/scene word-count distribution chart with color-band variance, tension tag row (setup/build/peak/release), 3 deterministic alert rules (low-tension run, length outlier, no tags), jump-to-scene from alerts, before/after comparison snapshot, sensitivity control slider, empty states, a11y, and open questions for engineering.

## Design Spec: Chapter Pacing Board

### Overview
The Pacing Board is a story-level revision diagnostic tool. It gives writers a macro view of structural pacing issues (chapter length variance, tension progression gaps) with one-click navigation to fix them. Differentiator: deterministic, explainable alerts with before/after snapshot validation — not genre formulas, not AI.

---

### 1. Entry Point and Layout

**Access:** Pacing tab in the story-level navigation (alongside Outline, Worldbuilding, etc.)

**Board layout (top to bottom):**
1. Toolbar (sensitivity control, snapshot button)
2. Chapter/Scene Distribution visualization
3. Tension Tag Row
4. Pacing Alert Panel
5. Comparison Snapshot (collapsed by default, expands after snapshot is taken)

---

### 2. Chapter/Scene Distribution Visualization

**Display:** Horizontal bar chart — one row per chapter, subdivided by scene
- Bar width proportional to word count
- Color bands:
  - Green = within acceptable length range
  - Yellow = 25-50% above/below story median
  - Red = >50% above/below story median
- Hover/tap on a segment: tooltip showing chapter/scene name, word count, tension tag

**Sidebar stats (right column):**
- Story median chapter length
- Shortest chapter / Longest chapter
- % of chapters within band

**Sensitivity control (toolbar):**
- Slider: Tight | Moderate | Loose (adjusts the variance thresholds)
- Default: Moderate
- Changing sensitivity re-evaluates all alerts in real time

---

### 3. Tension Tag Row

**Below the distribution chart:**
- One row per scene (ordered by chapter > scene position)
- Scene name (truncated) + tag selector
- Tag options: setup | build | peak | release | untagged
- Tag shown as color-coded pill: blue=setup, orange=build, red=peak, green=release, gray=untagged
- Click tag to change; keyboard accessible

**Chapter-level pacing summary:**
- Below each chapter section, show the tag distribution for that chapter: e.g., setup x2, build x3, peak x1
- If a chapter has no peak or release scenes, show a yellow indicator

---

### 4. Pacing Alert Panel

**Deterministic alert rules:**

Alert A — Long low-tension run:
- Trigger: 4+ consecutive scenes tagged setup or build with no peak or release
- Message: [N] consecutive low-tension scenes in [Chapter X]. Consider adding a tension peak or escalation.
- Severity: warning (yellow)

Alert B — Chapter length outlier:
- Trigger: Chapter word count is >50% above or below the story median
- Message: Chapter [X] is [N]% [longer/shorter] than your story average. This may create a pacing bump for readers.
- Severity: warning (yellow)

Alert C — No tension tags:
- Trigger: Story has fewer than 30% of scenes tagged
- Message: Tag your scenes with tension types to unlock pacing gap detection.
- Severity: info (blue)

**Alert card design:**
- Each alert card: icon + message + chapter/scene reference (hyperlinked to jump to that scene)
- Dismiss button (hides for this session; re-appears if underlying data changes)
- Max 8 alerts shown; prioritized by severity then chapter order

---

### 5. Jump-to-Scene from Alert

**Click chapter/scene reference link in an alert:**
- Navigates to that scene in the editor with the scene highlighted
- Pacing board remains accessible via back navigation or split panel (if supported)

---

### 6. Comparison Snapshot

**Take Snapshot button (toolbar):**
- Records current alert count and chapter length distribution as a baseline
- Snapshot badge shown: Snapshot taken [timestamp]
- After making edits and returning to Pacing board: Comparison section expands showing:
  - Before vs After alert counts per alert type
  - Before vs After bar chart overlay (ghost bars for original distribution)
  - Summary: X alerts resolved, Y chapters moved into acceptable range

**Limit:** One snapshot per story session; replaced on next snapshot

---

### 7. Empty States

- No scenes yet: Add chapters and scenes to your story to see pacing analysis.
- Scenes exist but none tagged: Use the tension tag row above to tag your scenes and unlock gap detection.
- All alerts dismissed, no new issues: Your pacing looks good. Keep writing!

---

### 8. Accessibility and Performance
- All chart elements have text equivalents (screen reader summary of distribution)
- Alert cards are focusable and keyboard operable
- Board computes distribution from existing word-count data; no additional processing required
- Tension tags stored per-scene; board recalculates on each open (no stale cache)
- Board is read-only on mobile; tag editing available via scene detail on mobile

---

### 9. Open Questions for Engineering
1. Word count per scene: is this already tracked and accessible, or does it need to be computed?
2. Tension tag storage: new field on scene entity or separate tag table?
3. Snapshot: client-side session storage or persisted per story?
4. Jump-to-scene: does the router support deep-linking to a specific scene by ID?

---

### Handoff
Design complete. Ready for engineering.

- Source issue: [BIN-121](/BIN/issues/BIN-121)
Recovery ticket; previous BIN-111 stuck (checkout 409) and cancelled.
